paragraph 1  
to analyze a list of historical dates and return only those that fall within a specified century, we can create a python function that processes the input dates and filters them based on the century. the function will take a list of date strings and an integer representing the century. the first step is to parse each date string into its year component. this can be done by splitting the string on the hyphen character and extracting the last part, which represents the year. once we have the year, we can check if it falls within the range of the specified century. for example, if the century is 19, the years will be from 1801 to 1900. the function will then filter the dates that fall within this range and return them as a list. this approach ensures that the function is efficient and can handle large datasets, as it processes each date in constant time. the use of string manipulation and conditional checks allows for a clear and concise implementation. the function will also handle edge cases, such as dates that fall exactly on the start or end of the century. by using this method, researchers can easily extract the desired dates from their dataset.
